title: The Turning-Point Gate

features.name: the gate itself
features.desc-on-touch: You stand at the point where the trail divides—both paths lead home but not to the same home. The gate remembers both choices as one.

features.name: left-path cottage
features.desc-on-touch: A small weathered cottage where the left path leads—it holds the shape of children who returned as themselves. Someone is still inside, sorting what they brought home.

features.name: right-path cottage
features.desc-on-touch: A cottage down the right path, shaped like the first but tilted—children came home here too, but speaking a half-remembered language, carrying objects from a field that never existed the same way twice.

features.name: dust-layer
features.desc-on-touch: Between the paths, footprints in dust—you can count them, but the counting changes each time. Some lead both directions at once.

features.name: fence-markers
features.desc-on-touch: Wire fence posts mark each path with names carved long ago; one name set, two lives, or one life split twice. You read them only if you know which path you took.

features.name: sign
features.desc-on-touch: A weathered post at the gate with faded words burned into it—one side says 'back toward town,' the other says the same thing. You stand between them.

inscription: both paths home; one home; the children chose twice

file.name: Convergence_Children_Field_Exit_1994.mp3
file.note: field recording of footsteps at a fork in a trail; at the 3:17 mark a voice says 'which way' and does not wait for an answer

exits.label: back to the next field, retracing the children's approach
the left path, where children came home as themselves
exits.to: NEW: the house where they sorted what they brought back

exits.label: the right path, where children came home changed
exits.to: NEW: the room where changed children sleep without dreaming

exits.label: toward the field itself, where the choice was first made
exits.to: NEW: the field before the gate, where two children were born
